_comment: data/horologion/orthros-kathisma.json — Horologion v5.7
PURPOSE: Appointment table for the two Psalter kathismata read at
ordinary weekday Orthros (Matins). Keyed by weekday (JS Date.getDay()).
RULE: Standard Byzantine Psalter weekly cycle for Orthros (not Vespers).
SOURCE: OCA/Antiochian Typikon; Jordanville Horologion (2008 edition).
The Orthros kathisma cycle is wholly distinct from the Vespers cycle.
At Vespers only one kathisma is read (Mon–Fri); at Orthros two are read
(Mon–Sat ordinary). Do not conflate these tables.
RELATIONSHIP TO VESPERS-KATHISMA.JSON:
Vespers reads Kathismata 1, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 (Mon–Sat one each).
Orthros reads Kathismata 4–15 in pairs across Mon–Sat, plus
Kathisma 2 or Polyeleos on Sunday. These are separate datasets.
KATHISMA PAIRS (ordinary weekday, non-vigil Orthros):
Monday    — Kathismata 4 + 5   (Ps 24–31 + Ps 32–36 LXX)
Tuesday   — Kathismata 6 + 7   (Ps 37–45 + Ps 46–54 LXX)
Wednesday — Kathismata 8 + 9   (Ps 55–63 + Ps 64–69 LXX)
Thursday  — Kathismata 10 + 11 (Ps 70–76 + Ps 77–84 LXX)
Friday    — Kathismata 12 + 13 (Ps 85–91 + Ps 92–100 LXX)
Saturday  — Kathismata 14 + 15 (Ps 101–108 + Ps 109–117 LXX)
NOTE: At the Saturday All-Night Vigil, Kathisma 17 (Ps 118) is
assigned at Orthros in place of ordinary practice. This file encodes
ordinary non-vigil Saturday only.
Sunday    — See note below.
SUNDAY NOTE:
On Sundays when the Polyeleos is appointed (Ps 134–135), the ordinary
Kathisma reading is replaced. On non-Polyeleos Sundays the assignment
varies by local use (some sources: Kathisma 2; others: see Typikon).
Sunday is explicitly deferred in this baseline. No appointment is
fabricated.
GREAT LENT:
During Great Lent the entire Psalter is read twice weekly, resulting
in a substantially expanded kathisma distribution (up to 3 kathismata
per service on some days). The ordinary cycle given here does not apply
during Great Lent. This baseline renders an honest Lenten rubric note
instead of a false ordinary-cycle assignment.
BRIGHT WEEK (Pascha–Thomas Saturday):
No kathisma is read. The Paschal Canon replaces all Psalter reading.
An explicit omission rubric is returned.
OVERRIDES NOT IMPLEMENTED (explicitly noted, not silently absent):
- Great Feasts: kathisma typically replaced by Polyeleos or omitted
- All-Night Vigil (Saturday): Kathisma 17 (Ps 118) at Matins
- Sunday: practice varies; explicitly deferred
- Feast-rank engine: deferred to a future pass
WEEKDAY INDEX: JS Date.getDay() — 0=Sunday, 1=Monday, 6=Saturday.

assignments.note: Sunday Orthros kathisma practice varies by occasion. On Sundays with the Polyeleos (Ps 134–135), the ordinary kathisma reading is supplanted. On non-Polyeleos Sundays some sources appoint Kathisma 2. Practice depends on whether a vigil was held on Saturday. Sunday Orthros kathisma is explicitly deferred in this baseline.

kathismaFirst.title: Fourth Kathisma
kathismaFirst.incipit: To Thee, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul

kathismaSecond.title: Fifth Kathisma
kathismaSecond.incipit: Rejoice in the Lord, O ye righteous

kathismaFirst.title: Sixth Kathisma
kathismaFirst.incipit: Rebuke me not, O Lord, in Thine anger

kathismaSecond.title: Seventh Kathisma
kathismaSecond.incipit: O clap your hands, all ye nations

kathismaFirst.title: Eighth Kathisma
kathismaFirst.incipit: Have mercy on me, O God, for man hath trodden me down

kathismaSecond.title: Ninth Kathisma
kathismaSecond.incipit: A hymn becometh Thee, O God, in Sion

kathismaFirst.title: Tenth Kathisma
kathismaFirst.incipit: In Thee, O Lord, have I hoped

kathismaSecond.title: Eleventh Kathisma
kathismaSecond.incipit: Give ear, O my people, to my law

kathismaFirst.title: Twelfth Kathisma
kathismaFirst.incipit: Incline Thine ear, O Lord, and hear me

kathismaSecond.title: Thirteenth Kathisma
kathismaSecond.incipit: It is good to give thanks unto the Lord
kathismaSecond.note: Some editions separate Ps 91 (LXX) into Kathisma 12 or begin Kathisma 13 at Ps 92. The above follows the standard OCA/Jordanville enumeration.

kathismaFirst.title: Fourteenth Kathisma
kathismaFirst.incipit: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto Thee

kathismaSecond.title: Fifteenth Kathisma
kathismaSecond.incipit: The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou at my right hand

assignments.saturday_note: At the Saturday All-Night Vigil, Kathisma 17 (Psalm 118 LXX) is appointed at Orthros in place of this ordinary assignment. This file encodes ordinary non-vigil Saturday Orthros only.

kathisma_extended_meta._comment: Kathismata not covered by the ordinary weekday assignments above (K4–K15). Also redefines K14–K15 to OCA standard psalm ranges for Lenten appointment resolution (the ordinary assignments table uses condensed non-standard ranges for K14–K15). SOURCE: OCA Psalter division — oca.org/liturgics/outlines/the-division-of-the-psalter-into-kathismas.
kathisma_extended_meta.title: First Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: Blessed is the man who hath not walked in the counsel of the ungodly

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Second Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: I will give thanks unto Thee, O Lord, with my whole heart

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Fourteenth Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come unto Thee
kathisma_extended_meta.note: OCA standard K14 = Ps 101–104 LXX (staseis: 101–102, 103, 104). The ordinary weekday Orthros table in this file uses a condensed assignment spanning Ps 101–108. This OCA-standard entry is used for Great Lent and Holy Week appointment resolution only.

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Fifteenth Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: O give thanks unto the Lord, for He is good; for His mercy endureth forever
kathisma_extended_meta.note: OCA standard K15 = Ps 105–108 LXX (staseis: 105, 106, 107–108). The ordinary weekday Orthros table in this file uses 'K15' to denote Ps 109–117. This OCA-standard entry is used for Great Lent and Holy Week appointment resolution only.

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Sixteenth Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou at my right hand

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Seventeenth Kathisma (The Great Kathisma / Amōmos)
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: Blessed are the undefiled in the way, who walk in the law of the Lord
kathisma_extended_meta.note: The Great Kathisma (Amōmos). Psalm 118 LXX is the longest Psalm in the Psalter, read in three staseis. Appointed at Great Saturday Orthros as the sole kathisma; also read on Great Lent Saturdays paired with Kathisma 16.

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Nineteenth Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: Praise ye the name of the Lord, praise Him, O ye servants of the Lord

kathisma_extended_meta.title: Twentieth Kathisma
kathisma_extended_meta.incipit: Blessed be the Lord, my strength, who teacheth my hands to war

_comment: Great Lent and Holy Week kathisma appointments at Orthros.
SOURCE: OCA (oca.org/liturgics/outlines/kathisma-readings-at-matins),
cross-checked against saintjonah.org/rub/kathismata.htm.
Kathisma numbers follow OCA standard 20-kathisma Psalter division.
Metadata for K1, K2, K14–K17, K19–K20 in kathisma_extended_meta above.
Metadata for K4–K13 reused from ordinary assignments array (same ranges).

weeks_1_to_4_and_6._comment: Great Lent Weeks 1, 2, 3, 4, and 6 (lentWeek !== 5). Three kathismata Mon–Fri; two on Saturday.

week_5._comment: Great Lent Week 5 only (lentWeek === 5). Thursday has only one kathisma; all others unchanged except Tuesday and Wednesday.

holy_week._comment: Holy Week Monday–Saturday. Great Thursday and Great Friday have no Psalter reading at Orthros. Great Saturday reads only Kathisma 17 (the Amōmos, Ps 118 LXX).

great-thursday.note: No Psalter reading. The kathismata are not appointed at Orthros on Great Thursday.

great-friday.note: No Psalter reading. The kathismata are not appointed at Orthros on Great Friday.
